cooperates.cooperate_desc: Hayabusa works best in setup + burst team compositions, where allies create crowd control or distraction, allowing him to isolate and instantly eliminate priority targets, but struggles against heavy crowd control and tightly grouped tank compositions.

counters.counter_desc: Hayabusa is strongest against squishy, uncoordinated teams, but struggles heavily vs hard crowd control heroes and suppression-based tanks, where shadow mobility and burst timing can be completely shut down.

dominants.dominant_desc: This build is made for fast burst kills + shadow escape + snowball domination, letting Hayabusa delete backline heroes instantly and reset fights safely.

emblems.emblem_desc: Used for high burst damage, penetration, and snowball potential to instantly delete backline targets and dominate early-to-mid game.

equips.equip_desc:
➤Hunter Strike - movement + burst rotation
➤ Heptaseas - first hit explosion damage
➤ Malefic Roar - tank shred
➤ Endless Battle - sustain + true damage
➤for extra damage (snowball)
    Blade of Despair (swap Endless Battle)
➤ Immortality - second chance in late fights


This build is made for fast burst kills + shadow escape + snowball domination, letting Hayabusa delete backline heroes instantly and reset fights safely.

hero.hero_overview:
Hayabusa - HERO ACADEMY OVERVIEW
(Mobile Legends Bang Bang)
- Role- Assassin (Hyper Carry)
- Lane- Jungle
- Specialty- (Burst - Chase )
- Identity
- Backline executioner
- Single-target killer
- High mobility assassin
- Core Function
- Eliminate MM/Mage
- Split push lanes
- Clean fights (last entry)
( Jungle Route )
-- Blue - Litho - Red - Camp - Gold lane gank
- Rotation
- Early ( Farm - Level 4 - Gank - Turtle)
- Mid ( Jungle - Pickoff - Push - Repeat)
- Late ( Hide - One-shot - Escape - Reset)
- Fight Rule
- Second entry only
- Target isolated enemy
- Avoid teamfight center
( Priority )
- Core kill
- Objective secure
- Lane pressure
- Jungle invade
- Weakness
- Crowd control
- Group fights
-Tanky enemies
(Summary)
- High-mobility assassin focused on precise timing, isolated kills, and map pressure control

1- Extremely high single-target damage
2-Insane mobility (shadow teleport)
3-Strong split push (can pressure lanes alone)
4-Great for solo carry in ranked
hero.hero_weakness:
1-Weak vs crowd control (CC)
2-Struggles against grouped enemies
3-Requires good timing & decision making
4- Not effective in 5 v 5 front fights

cooperates.cooperate_desc:
Hayabusa synergizes extremely well with Angela because of their strong burst and protection combo. Angela Ultimate can attach to Hayabusa while he dives into the enemy backline, providing shields, extra damage, and crowd control.

This allows Hayabusa to safely execute his one-shot combo without being easily killed. Angela also helps sustain him during risky engagements, making his assassination more consistent. Together, they excel at picking off squishy targets and escaping safely, especially in mid to late game fights.

counters.counter_desc:
Hayabusa has a strong advantage against Floryn because Floryn lacks mobility and is very vulnerable to burst damage.

Hayabusa can easily dive into the backline and eliminate Floryn before she can react or fully support her team. Even though Floryn provides healing, it is often not enough to survive Hayabusa full combo, especially with a one-shot build. By removing Floryn early, Hayabusa weakens the enemy team's sustain and increases the chance of winning team fights.

dominants.dominant_desc:
In the mid to late game, this build reaches its full power and can instantly eliminate squishy enemies. Focus on targeting enemy marksmen, mages, or low-defense heroes to maximize the one-shot potential.

During laning, play safely and farm efficiently until core items like Hunter Strike and Blade of Despair are completed. Avoid unnecessary fights early unless you have a clear advantage.

In team fights, do not engage first. Wait for your tank or initiator to start, then flank from the side or backline. Use your full combo quickly to burst down priority targets, then disengage immediately using the movement speed from Hunter Strike.

Always maintain good positioning and vision. Bush camping and surprise attacks are key to this playstyle. Avoid prolonged fights since this build lacks sustain and durability.

Against tanky enemies, rely on Malefic Roar to deal damage effectively, but prioritize eliminating squishy heroes first.

equips.equip_desc: the goal is high physical penetration + burst damage, so your combo kills enemies in 1-2 seconds.

hero.hero_overview: As the second heir to the Shadow Sect of the Scarlet Shadow, Hayabusa had always ...

spell.spell_desc: Deals 520 True Damage to a creeps and minions.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: These heroes synergize with Hayabusa by providing crowd control, initiation, and frontline pressure. This allows Hayabusa to safely target and eliminate backline enemies during fights without being interrupted.

counters.counter_desc: Counter heroes like Khufra, Chou, and Saber are effective against Hayabusa because they can stop his mobility, interrupt his combo, or burst him down quickly before he escapes or secures kills.

dominants.dominant_desc: Hayabusa is strongest in mid to late game after core items. Farm safely in early game until you get items. In mid game, rotate and gank isolated enemies, especially marksmen and mages. In fights, wait for enemy CC, then target the backline with ultimate and escape using shadows. In late game, play as an assassin, focus on picking key targets, and avoid long fights.

emblems.emblem_desc: I chose the Assassin Emblem because Hayabusa is a burst assassin who needs high damage and fast kills in the jungle. The attributes are focused on increasing physical penetration and damage against isolated targets, which helps him eliminate squishy heroes quickly. The Killing Spree talent also gives HP recovery and movement speed after a kill, allowing Hayabusa to stay alive and continue snowballing in fights and ganks.

equips.equip_desc: I chose this equipment build because Hayabusa is a burst assassin who needs high damage, penetration, and mobility to quickly eliminate enemy backline heroes. The build provides strong early-game damage for jungle clearing and ganks, mid-game burst to secure kills, and late-game finishing power against squishy targets. Items like Hunter Strike and Blade of the Heptaseas help with early snowballing, while Endless Battle and Blade of Despair maximize burst damage. Malefic Roar ensures effectiveness against tanky enemies, allowing Hayabusa to stay relevant throughout all stages of the game.

This build focuses on high burst damage and fast assassination potential. It helps Hayabusa clear jungle quickly, snowball in early game, and instantly eliminate squishy heroes in mid to late game. Hunter Strike and Heptaseas give strong early damage and mobility, while Endless Battle and Blade of Despair maximize burst damage. Malefic Roar ensures damage against tanks and high armor enemies.

hero.hero_overview: Hayabusa is a high-mobility assassin who specializes in quick burst damage and eliminating backline targets. He relies on shadow skills to move in and out of fights safely. His main strength is picking off squishy heroes like marksmen and mages, especially in the mid to late game when he has items.

	3:	Strong split pushing and map pressure
	4:	Can easily delete squishy heroes in seconds
	5:	Hard to catch when played correctly

1:  Weak in early game before items

	4:	Needs good energy management
	5:	Not effective in long team fights

counters.counter_desc: Hayabusa is at a huge disadvantage against Franco because of his suppression ultimate Bloody Hunt. This skill instantly locks Hayabusa in place and prevents him from using any shadows to escape. Franco is also a very tanky hero making it impossible for Hayabusa to kill him with a single combo. If Franco lands his hook or uses his ultimate during a team fight Hayabusa becomes an easy target for the entire enemy team. The only way to survive is to wait for Franco to waste his skills on someone else before jumping in.

dominants.dominant_desc: Hayabusa reaches his maximum potential during the mid and late game stages once he has his core items. Focus on split pushing lanes to create map pressure and force the enemy team to divide their forces. In team fights do not be the first to jump in. Wait for the enemy to exhaust their crowd control abilities then use your shadows to dive the backline and eliminate the marksman or mage. Always maintain your energy levels by securing the purple buff and use your ultimate strategically on isolated targets to ensure the full damage hits one hero. If the situation becomes dangerous use your shadows to instantly retreat and reposition.

emblems.emblem_desc: The Assassin Emblem set is the best choice for Hayabusa to deal with both tanks and squishy targets. Use Agility in the first tier for maximum movement speed to reach objectives and lanes quickly. Select Seasoned Hunter in the second tier to clear the jungle and secure the Turtle or Lord faster. For the core talent pick War Cry to boost your damage output by eight percent after landing basic attacks or skills. This setup provides the necessary mobility for ganking and extra sustained damage to eliminate any type of enemy hero during a fight.

equips.equip_desc: Best build for Hayabusa. Hunter Strike helps to clear the jungle quickly, deal initial damage, and build the early game. War Axe improves survivability and provides spell vamp and true damage. Malefic Roar is a mandatory purchase in the current meta because tanks and fighters are the main problem for Hayabusa at the start. Then follows Blade of Despair, the core item for the hero, which maximizes damage output. Endless Battle fits this hero well by increasing total damage and true damage while enhancing survivability and movement speed.

hero.hero_overview:
Core Buff: Always prioritize Purple (Blue) Buff to manage high energy consumption.
Pathing: Start at Purple Buff - Lithowanderer - Orange Buff -Reach Level 4 as fast as possible.

Invasion: Use shadows to safely steal enemy creeps and secure neutral objectives (Turtle/Lord) with Retribution
Hayabusa excels at picking off backline squishies and split-pushing. His core mechanic relies on Quad Shadow for outplaying enemies and Ougi: Shadow Kill for invulnerable burst damage.
hero.hero_strength:
Laning/Farming: Exceptional wave clear and jungle clearing speed. High mobility allows for safe counter-jungling and stealing enemy buffs using Quad Shadow.
Team Fight: excels at backline diving. Can easily target and eliminate enemy Marksmen or Mages while remaining invulnerable during his Ultimate.
Game Phase:
Early/Mid: Strong snowball potential after reaching Level 4.
Late: One of the best split-pushers in the game; can pressure lanes and escape any gank.
Characteristics: High outplay potential, elusive movement, and massive single-target Burst Damage.
hero.hero_weakness:
Laning/Farming: Highly dependent on the Purple (Blue) Buff. Without it, his energy regenerates too slowly, making him nearly useless in extended fights.
Team Fight: Vulnerable to Hard Crowd Control (CC). If stunned or suppressed (e.g., by Franco or Kaja) before casting shadows, he is very squishy and easily killed.
Game Phase:
Early: Weak before reaching Level 4; can be bullied or invaded by early-game aggressive junglers (like Martis or Thamuz).
Team Fights: His Ultimate damage is split among multiple targets. He struggles in 5v5 clashes if he cannot isolate a single enemy.
Characteristics: High skill cap (requires fast fingers and precise shadow placement). Very fragile (low HP) and punished heavily for any positioning mistakes.

data.recommend: Master the art of shadows with this ultimate Hayabusa guide. Learn how to dominate the jungle with Flame Retribution and execute perfect combos to eliminate priority targets. This guide covers the bes

spell.spell_desc: Flame Retribution steals Physical Attack from the opponent and transfers it to you. This significantly boosts your Ultimate burst damage while simultaneously weakening the target. Hayabusa gains a decisive advantage in duels against Marksmen and Mages because they lose a portion of their strength. Use the spell at the start of your combo so that all subsequent skills deal maximum damage. This is the best option for achieving peak assassin efficiency and quickly eliminating priority targets.

hero.hero_overview: Hayabusa is a hero with high burst damage potential. This hero is mainly suitable for the jungle role. Farm creeps as fast as you can to reach level 4 and unlock your ultimate. Start according to your side lanes. If gold lane is top, start from your red buff then progress towards blue buff then invade gold lane. Use your second skill to tag the enemy. Use first skill to put marka on the enemy increasing your damage and burst potential.
hero.hero_strength: This hero excels in all phases in the entire game. Though the burst potential in the early game is low so you need to keep farming to increase your damage potential in the mid till late game. Hayabusa excels in taking out the back lines. Use his skill two to mark the enemy damage dealers, put marks using his first skills then your use ultimate while you're near that enemy.
hero.hero_weakness: Hayabusa's weakness are other burst type heroes. For example: Saber, kagura etc. Burst him down quickly before he takes out your damage dealers. For equipment, use winter crown to freeze while he uses his ultimate or use antique curass if you're a tank to get enough sustainability.

spell.spell_desc: Hayabusa is mainly a jungler so retribution is a must!

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Hayabusa works best with heroes who have strong crowd control or pick-off skills, because they can lock or isolate enemies, allowing him to use his ultimate effectively and secure quick kills.

counters.counter_desc: Hayabusa works best with heroes who have strong crowd control or pick-off skills, because they can lock or isolate enemies, allowing him to use his ultimate effectively and secure quick kills.

dominants.dominant_desc: Hayabusa's dominant phase is the mid to late game, when he finally gets the items and levels he needs to deal real damage. During the early game, his burst is limited and he mainly focuses on farming and avoiding unnecessary fights. Once he reaches his core items and unlocks higher skill levels, his mobility, burst damage, and split-push potential increase massively. In the mid game, he can already eliminate isolated enemies, and by the late game, he becomes extremely dangerous to marksmen and mages, controlling the map through constant pressure and quick assassinations.
dominants.dominant_title: Mid - Late Game Power Spike Phase

emblems.emblem_desc: War Cry Works Best With His Passive

equips.equip_desc: Crazy Execution power with sustainability

hero.hero_overview:
Hayabusa is a high-mobility Assassin who specializes in burst damage and quick eliminations. Using shadows, he can dive into enemy lines, defeat isolated targets, and escape before taking damage. His playstyle focuses on timing, positioning, and precise execution rather than direct team fighting.
He excels at split pushing and hunting backline heroes such as Marksmen and Mages. When properly farmed, Hayabusa becomes extremely difficult to catch and can control the map through constant pressure.
However, he is weak during the early game and heavily dependent on items. His Ultimate is most effective against isolated enemies, and crowd control effects can shut him down quickly if mistimed.
Hayabusa rewards mechanical skill and decision-making, making him one of the strongest late-game assassins when mastered.
hero.hero_strength: Hayabusa's strength is pure fear. He can appear from anywhere, delete squishy heroes in one blink, and vanish before backup arrives. His mobility is insane, his split push is insane, and if he gets farmed, even tanks start respecting him. In late game, one mistake from the enemy marksman means instant noodles for him and a free highlight clip.
hero.hero_weakness: But this ninja is also allergic to crowd control. One stun and his whole career collapses. Early game he hits like a wet shuriken, his ultimate cries when minions show up, and if he falls behind in gold, he becomes a shadow throwing motivational damage only.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Hayabusa is a versatile and powerful assassin in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, known for his high mobility and burst damage. Here are some tips to help you master Hayabusa:

Skills and Abilities: Hayabusa's skills apply a stack of Shadow Mark on hit, which increases his damage output. His Ninjutsu: Phantom Shuriken skill throws three shurikens that deal physical damage and slow enemies, regenerating energy on hit and gaining Spell Vamp as it levels up.
 His Ninjutsu: Quad Shadow creates four shadows around him, allowing him to teleport to each shadow once and deal damage while slowing enemies.
 His ultimate, Ougi: Shadow Kill, makes him invisible and deals damage to a single target up to nine times.
Build and Emblem: Equip Swift Boots for increased attack speed to stack Hayabusa's passive faster. For items, consider Ice Retribution for jungle farming speed, Hunter Strike for stacking damage, Malefic Roar for physical penetration, Blade of Despair for increased damage on low HP enemies, and Endless Battle for true damage after casting a skill.
 The best emblem for Hayabusa is the Assassin Emblem, which provides Adaptive Penetration, Adaptive Attack, and Movement Speed.
Battle Spells: Retribution is the best battle spell for Hayabusa, especially if he is played as a jungler, as it boosts jungle farming speed and objective control.
 Execute can be used for more damage if needed.
Gameplay Tips: Focus on manipulating shadows and using skills to maximize damage and mobility. Use Phantom Shuriken frequently in the laning phase to poke enemies and slow them down. Deploy shadows strategically to engage enemies from unexpected angles and escape ganks.
 Combine Quad Shadow with Phantom Shuriken to maximize damage and mobility in skirmishes.
Farm and Jungle: As a jungler, Hayabusa can farm quickly and safely, leading to early power spikes and map dominance.
 Use Retribution to boost jungle farming speed and objective

hero.hero_overview: Hayabusa is an assassin in Mobile Legends who excels at dealing high burst damage, making him a formidable opponent against tanks.
hero.hero_strength: Hayabusa is a powerful assassin in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, known for his exceptional mobility and burst damage. His strength can be rated highly, typically around 8-9 out of 10, due to his ability to quickly eliminate squishy targets and escape dangerous situations.
hero.hero_weakness:
Hayabusa, a hero in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, has several weaknesses that players often exploit. He is weak in team fights and can be easily killed when he rushes in. Additionally, his ultimate skill can be blocked by certain heroes and items, such as Minsittha's ultimate and Winter Truncheon, which makes him invulnerable to damage.
 Hayabusa also struggles against heroes with crowd control (CC) and suppression skills, as these can immobilize him and prevent him from escaping or attacking effectively.
 Furthermore, his long cooldowns and dependence on skills make him vulnerable if his abilities are on cooldown, limiting his ability to engage in combat.
 His low health and lack of high attack speed also contribute to his fragility.

hero.hero_strength: Hayabusa is one of those heroes whose kit is great on paper but falls short in practice. He also isn't helped by the healer support meta that encourages teams to clump up together. Yet, with a few small tweaks, he can be a strong pick again

hero.hero_overview:
Hayabusa adalah salah satu hero Mobile Legends: Bang Bang (MLBB) yang berjenis Assassin (Pembunuh). Ia adalah seorang ninja yang memiliki kemampuan untuk bergerak cepat dan menyerang musuh dengan cepat dan tepat.

Hayabusa memiliki kemampuan unik seperti:

- Kemampuan untuk bergerak cepat dan tidak terlihat oleh musuh.
- Kemampuan untuk menyerang musuh dengan cepat dan tepat.
- Kemampuan untuk menghindari serangan musuh dengan mudah.

Hayabusa adalah salah satu hero yang paling populer di MLBB karena kemampuan dan kecepatannya yang sangat tinggi.
hero.hero_strength:
Berikut adalah kekuatan Hayabusa di Mobile Legends:

*Kekuatan Utama*
1. *Kecepatan*: Hayabusa memiliki kecepatan gerak yang sangat tinggi, memungkinkannya untuk mengejar musuh atau menghindari serangan dengan mudah.
2. *Kemampuan Stealth*: Hayabusa dapat menjadi tidak terlihat oleh musuh, memungkinkannya untuk melakukan serangan tiba-tiba atau menghindari serangan musuh.
3. *Serangan Cepat*: Hayabusa memiliki serangan yang sangat cepat dan tepat, memungkinkannya untuk melakukan damage yang besar pada musuh.

*Kemampuan Pasif*
1. *Phantom Slash*: Hayabusa memiliki kemampuan pasif yang memungkinkannya untuk melakukan serangan tambahan pada musuh yang terkena serangannya.
2. *Shadow Dance*: Hayabusa dapat meningkatkan kecepatan geraknya dan mengurangi cooldown kemampuan-kemampuannya ketika berada di dekat musuh.

*Kemampuan Aktif*
1. *Quad Shadow Slash*: Hayabusa dapat melakukan serangan pada empat arah yang berbeda, memungkinkannya untuk melakukan damage yang besar pada musuh.
2. *Shadow Slip*: Hayabusa dapat bergerak cepat ke arah yang diinginkan, memungkinkannya untuk menghindari serangan musuh atau mengejar musuh.
3. *Ougi: Shadow Kill*: Hayabusa dapat melakukan serangan yang sangat kuat pada musuh, memungkinkannya untuk melakukan damage yang sangat besar pada musuh.
hero.hero_weakness:
Berikut adalah kelemahan Hayabusa di Mobile Legends:

*Kelemahan Utama*
1. *Kurangnya Durabilitas*: Hayabusa memiliki HP yang relatif rendah, membuatnya rentan terhadap serangan musuh.
2. *Ketergantungan pada Kemampuan*: Hayabusa sangat bergantung pada kemampuan-kemampuannya untuk melakukan damage dan menghindari serangan musuh.
3. *Kurangnya Crowd Control*: Hayabusa tidak memiliki kemampuan crowd control yang efektif

spell.spell_desc:
Berikut adalah beberapa retribusi yang cocok untuk Hayabusa di Mobile Legends:

*Retribusi Utama*
1. *Burst Damage*: Retribusi ini memungkinkan Hayabusa untuk melakukan damage yang besar pada musuh dalam waktu singkat.
2. *Assasination*: Retribusi ini memungkinkan Hayabusa untuk melakukan serangan yang cepat dan tepat pada musuh.

*Retribusi Alternatif*
1. *Life Steal*: Retribusi ini memungkinkan Hayabusa untuk mengembalikan HP-nya sendiri ketika menyerang musuh.
2. *Critical Damage*: Retribusi ini memungkinkan Hayabusa untuk melakukan damage yang lebih besar pada musuh ketika melakukan serangan kritikal.

hero.hero_overview: Hayabusa is a renowned ninja hailing from the famous Oboro clan. Trained in the art of stealth and speed, he carries the legacy of his family with pride. However, when his clan was betrayed and destroyed by a traitor, Hayabusa vowed to avenge his people. With unmatched agility and lethal skill in ninjutsu, he travels the world, seeking out the enemies responsible for his clan's downfall. His quest for justice is relentless, and his skills make him a formidable opponent in battle.
hero.hero_strength:
High mobility, allowing him to dodge enemy attacks and re-engage.
Strong burst damage with his ultimate and skills, especially against squishy targets.
Can deal continuous damage using his passive and ability rotation.
hero.hero_weakness:
Lack of crowd control makes him vulnerable to CC-heavy enemies.
Squishy in nature and can be easily killed if caught by enemies.
Requires skill and timing to fully utilize his abilities effectively.

data.recommend: Based on my gaming experience, I'll give you this guidelines

spell.spell_desc:
As a Jungler, Hayabusa relies on clearing jungle camps quickly to gain gold and experience. Retribution significantly helps him deal extra damage to jungle monsters, allowing him to clear camps faster. This increases his farming efficiency, enabling him to reach his core items faster and be more impactful in the mid-game.

When using Retribution, Hayabusa can also gain a speed boost after securing a jungle monster. This can help him move around the map quicker, making it easier to rotate, gank, or assist teammates in fights.

Retribution allows Hayabusa to secure major objectives like the Turtle, Lord, and buffs, which are essential for maintaining map control. This spell ensures he can quickly and efficiently take objectives, giving his team a strategic advantages.

Overall, Retribution is essential for Hayabusa to maximize his jungle potential, improve his map presence.

cooperates.cooperate_desc:
* Khufra:
   * Explanation: Khufra is an excellent tank that can create opportunities for Hayabusa. Khufra's abilities to crowd control and group enemies together allow Hayabusa to easily eliminate multiple targets with his ultimate.
   * Why they work together: Khufra sets up perfect engages for Hayabusa to unleash his full damage potential. Khufra's tankiness also draws enemy focus, allowing Hayabusa to operate more freely.

counters.counter_desc:
* Franco:
   * Explanation: Franco is a major threat to Hayabusa. His hook can pull Hayabusa out of position, disrupting his combos and making him vulnerable to burst damage.
   * Why he's a counter: Hayabusa relies on his agility and mobility to outmaneuver opponents. Franco's hook directly counters this, potentially leading to Hayabusa's quick demise.
 * Karrie:
   * Explanation: Karrie is a strong marksman who excels at dealing damage to high-health targets like Hayabusa. Her ultimate and high attack speed can quickly melt through Hayabusa's defenses.
   * Why she's a counter: Hayabusa's relatively squishy nature makes him susceptible to Karrie's consistent and percent-based damage. Her ability to suppress and chase also makes it difficult for Hayabusa to escape.
Additional Notes:
 * You can add more examples of heroes that synergize well with or counter Hayabusa.
 * Be sure to explain how and why these heroes work together or against each other.
 * Keep your language clear and concise for easy understanding.
Let me know if you'd like any other variations or specific examples!

dominants.dominant_desc:
Early Game Focus: Farming and Leveling
Starting Items:
 * Hunter's Knife: To increase jungle monster kill speed and accelerate the gold farming process.
Lane:
 * Mid Lane: It is the most suitable for Hayabusa in the early game. It allows him quick access to jungle monsters and the ability to easily support other lanes.
Farming Techniques:
 * Use Skill 1 (Shadow): To quickly clear minion waves.
 * Focus on last hitting minions: To get the maximum amount of gold.
 * Take advantage of skill downtime: To kill monsters in the jungle to increase gold and level.
Leveling Priority:
 * Skill 1 (Shadow): To increase damage and speed of clearing minion waves.
 * Skill 2 (Shadow): To increase maneuverability and escape ability.
Ganking Opportunities:
 * After reaching level four: Hayabusa can start looking for opportunities to gank in other lanes, especially the bottom lane.
 * Use Skill 2 (Shadow) and the ultimate (Shadow Kill): To quickly eliminate enemies.
Early Game Objectives:
 * Securing Buffs: Helping your team get jungle buffs.
 * Dropping the first turret: To gain an advantage on the map.
 * Participating in killing the Turtle: To get rewards for the team.
Laning Precautions:
 * Beware of enemy ambushes: Especially in the early game when Hayabusa's skills are limited.
 * Watch the map: To avoid falling into enemy ambushes.
Team Fight Strategies:
 * Focus on weak targets: Such as mages and marksmen.
 * Use the ultimate (Shadow Kill): To quickly eliminate enemies.
Additional Tips:
 * Practice using Hayabusa's skills: To get the most out of them.
 * Communicate with your team: To coordinate attacks and execute ambushes.
Important Notes:
 * This is just a template, you can modify it to suit your playstyle.
 * Remember that the balance between gold farming and participating in combat is the key to success with Hayabusa in the early game.
Good luck in your matches!

emblems.emblem_desc:
"I chose the War Cry emblem set for Hayabusa because it significantly enhances his damage-dealing capabilities and makes him a more effective assassin in combat."
Explanation of War Cry:
"The War Cry trait grants me an 8% increase in physical damage after every 3 attacks or skills that hit enemy heroes. This effect allows Hayabusa to quickly amplify his offensive power, making him a formidable threat in both 1v1 and team fights."
"This emblem is ideal for Hayabusa because he relies on a series of rapid and successive attacks to inflict damage. War Cry fits perfectly with his playstyle, as he can easily trigger the effect and increase his damage output significantly."
Comparison with other options:
"Of course, there are other options for Hayabusa emblem sets, such as Assassin or Festival of Blood. However, I find that War Cry offers the best balance between damage increase and survivability. It's an excellent choice for players who prefer an aggressive playstyle and want to maximize Hayabusa's abilities."
Conclusion:
"Overall, I am very satisfied with choosing the War Cry emblem set for Hayabusa. It helps me secure victories in matches and makes me feel like I am in control of the battlefield. I recommend this emblem to anyone looking to improve their Hayabusa performance."
Additional tips:
 * You can add more details about how to use War Cry effectively in different situations.
 * You can mention some heroes that War Cry is particularly effective against.
 * You can share some tips on how to use this emblem set with appropriate builds and spells.
I hope this explanation is helpful.

equips.equip_desc:
Equipment Name: Shadow Dance 
Equipment Choices:
 * Magic Boots: Swift Boots 
 * Hunter Strike
 * Sky Piercer 
 * Malefic Roar 
 *Rose Gold meteor
 * Blade of Despair 
Explanation:
This Hayabusa build focuses on maximizing burst damage and mobility, allowing him to quickly eliminate priority targets and escape unscathed.
 * Swift Boots: These boots provide a significant boost to movement speed, enabling Hayabusa to roam the map quickly and engage enemies from unexpected angles.
 * Hunter Strike: This item synergizes perfectly with Hayabusa's kit, granting additional physical penetration after each kill or assist. This allows him to snowball quickly and dominate the early to mid-game.
 * Sky Piercer: This item further enhances Hayabusa's physical penetration, making him incredibly effective against tanky enemies.
 * Malefic Roar: This item provides a massive boost to physical attack, allowing Hayabusa to melt through enemy defenses.
 * Endless Battle: This item provides a mix of useful stats, including physical lifesteal, cooldown reduction, and true damage. The true damage passive is particularly useful for dealing with high-health targets.
 * Blade of Despair: This item is the final piece of the puzzle, providing a huge damage boost against low-health enemies. This allows Hayabusa to secure kills with ease and snowball out of control.
This build is best suited for aggressive players who like to play the role of an assassin. With his high mobility and burst damage, Hayabusa can easily pick off enemy carries and disrupt team fights. However, it's important to be mindful of positioning and cooldowns, as Hayabusa can be vulnerable when his skills are on cooldown.
Remember to adjust the build and explanation based on your own playstyle and preferences.

hero.hero_overview:
Hayabusa is an assassin who is known for his high mobility and burst damage. He is one of the most popular heroes in the game, and is often used in both casual and competitive play.
Hayabusa's lore is as follows:
"In the far east, there is a land called the Land of Dawn. In this land, there is a ninja clan called the Shadow Sect. The Shadow Sect is a clan of highly skilled ninjas who are known for their loyalty and their skill in combat.
One of the most talented ninjas in the Shadow Sect was a young man named Hayabusa. Hayabusa was a prodigy, and he quickly rose through the ranks of the Shadow Sect. He was eventually chosen to be the leader of the Shadow Sect, and he led his clan to many victories.
Hayabusa was a skilled warrior, but he was also a wise and compassionate leader. He was loved and respected by his clan, and he was always willing to help those in need.
One day, a great evil came to the Land of Dawn. A powerful demon named the Shadow Demon attacked the Land of Dawn, and he threatened to destroy everything in his path.
Hayabusa and the Shadow Sect fought bravely against the Shadow Demon, but they were no match for his power. The Shadow Demon defeated the Shadow Sect, and he killed many of Hayabusa's friends and allies.
Hayabusa was devastated by the loss of his friends, and he vowed to avenge them. He trained for many years, and he eventually became strong enough to defeat the Shadow Demon.
Hayabusa defeated the Shadow Demon, and he saved the Land of Dawn from destruction. He was hailed as a hero, and he became a legend in the Land of Dawn."
Hayabusa is a powerful hero who is capable of carrying his team to victory. He is a skilled assassin who is able to quickly eliminate his targets. He is also a wise and compassionate leader who is always willing to help those in need.
hero.hero_strength:
. Here's a breakdown of his strengths and weaknesses, explained in clear, understandable English:
Hero Strength (Advantages)
 * Laning/Farming:
   * Wave Clear: Hayabusa excels at quickly eliminating minion waves, thanks to his abilities that allow him to dash between enemies and deal significant damage.
   * Mobility: He's incredibly fast, making it easy to rotate between lanes and reach farming areas quickly.
   * Escape Artist: Hayabusa can use his skills to escape from tricky situations or engage enemies unexpectedly, making him difficult to catch.
 * Team Fights:
   * Target Elimination: His ultimate allows him to single out and quickly eliminate vulnerable heroes like Marksmen or Mages.
   * Area Damage: Hayabusa's abilities can inflict high damage to multiple enemies in a small area, making him effective in team fights.
   * Untargetable: While using his skills, he becomes difficult to target, allowing him to stay in the fight longer and deal more damage.
 * Game Stages:
   * Early to Mid Game Dominance: Hayabusa is strongest in the early and mid-game phases, where he can use his wave clear and mobility to gain an advantage.
   * Late Game Potential: While his power might slightly decrease in the late game, he remains a threat in team fights and excels at picking off priority targets.
 * Other Strengths:
   * Assassin: Hayabusa specializes in quickly eliminating vulnerable heroes.
   * Elusive: His skills make him incredibly difficult for enemies to catch and lock down.
   * High Skill Cap: He's a fun and rewarding hero to play for those who enjoy a challenge.
hero.hero_weakness:
Hero Weaknesses (Disadvantages)
 * Laning/Farming:
   * Crowd Control Vulnerability: Heroes with abilities that can stun, freeze, or suppress him can severely limit his mobility and effectiveness.
   * Tanky Opponents: Hayabusa can struggle against heroes with high defense, as he relies on burst damage to secure kills.

spell.spell_desc:
The Battle Spell you have chosen is Retribution. Here are some reasons why you might choose this Battle Spell and some tips for using it with Hayabusa:
Reasons for choosing Retribution with Hayabusa:
 * Jungle Control: Retribution is essential for securing jungle buffs (like the blue and red buff) and for stealing buffs from the enemy team. Hayabusa, as an assassin, often roams the jungle, and Retribution helps him secure objectives quickly and efficiently.
 * Fast Farming: The passive effect of Retribution increases the experience and gold gained from jungle creeps, allowing Hayabusa to level up faster and acquire items more quickly.
 * Burst Damage: Retribution deals a significant amount of true damage to a single target, which can be useful for finishing off low-health enemies or for dealing extra damage to high-health targets like the Turtle or Lord.
Tips and Techniques for using Retribution with Hayabusa:
 * Time it Right: Pay attention to the cooldown of Retribution and use it wisely. Don't waste it on low-health creeps or use it too early when contesting objectives.
 * Combine with Skills: Use Retribution in combination with Hayabusa's skills to maximize damage output. For example, you can use his first skill, Phantom Shadow, to mark an enemy, then use Retribution to deal additional burst damage.
 * Secure Objectives: Use Retribution to secure important objectives like the Turtle and Lord. Time it so that you use it at the last moment to steal the objective from the enemy team.
 * Jungle Mobility: Use Retribution to quickly clear jungle camps and move between lanes. This allows you to farm efficiently and be ready to assist your team in ganks or team fights.
 * Enemy Awareness: Be aware of the enemy team's positioning and try to use Retribution when they are not expecting it. This can help you secure objectives or steal buffs without being contested.
Remember, practice makes perfect. Experiment with Retribution in different situations and find what works best

data.title: Advanced Tactics for Hayabusa"
